title: QUIC Transport Bridge, Diagnostics, and Sender/Recovery Ownership Architecture
purpose: Describe the main-library seams that keep TLS-facing transport facts, diagnostics emission, and sender/recovery ownership inside Incursa.Quic instead of the interop harness.
design_summary: The library adds three adjacent seam types. A TLS bridge state captures transport-visible facts such as local and peer transport parameters, key availability, handshake confirmation, key updates, and fatal alerts. A diagnostics sink abstraction keeps emitters format-agnostic and cheap or no-op when disabled. A sender/recovery runtime owner wraps the existing sender-flow controller and recovery controller so packet-sent state, PTO ownership, and retransmission planning stay in the transport layer where future hosts can reuse them.
risks: The new seam classes must stay architectural helpers rather than becoming a second TLS or recovery implementation.
Diagnostics plumbing should remain opt-in so the library still works cleanly in non-interop hosts.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This slice stays in the main library because it owns transport/runtime facts that any future host could reuse. The interop harness only consumes the seam later.
